goal: Stop Finder/WebDAV-created non-content records and missing local storage records from appearing in file lists that trigger preview 404s.
assumptions: Preview 404 means the backend cannot open the stored object for an otherwise active DB record.
macOS AppleDouble files whose names start with ._ are metadata/resource-fork files, not user preview content.
API file lists and WebDAV PROPFIND both flow through FileService list paths.
Local storage existence checks are acceptable at page-list granularity; remote storage checks should not be added to the hot list path.
risks: Filtering after DB pagination can under-fill a page if many records are orphaned.
Traditional pagination total can only be reduced by hidden records observed on the current page.
Existing frontend or Redis list caches may need a refetch after deployment.
Skipping S3 existence checks means remote orphan cleanup remains a maintenance concern.
steps: Confirm DB rows exist for reported IDs and physical local files are missing.
Add failing WebDAV AppleDouble ignore test.
Add failing API list missing-local-object filter test.
Ignore AppleDouble files in WebDAV PUT.
Hide historical AppleDouble rows in SQL and service list filtering.
Filter missing local storage objects from list responses with warning logs.
Bump file list cache visibility key.
Run focused integration tests and backend verification.
